properties.attack_risk: HIGH — US/West dependency; export controls used as weapon
properties.conflict_status: ACTIVE — China imposed REE export controls 2023-2025; escalating
properties.summary: China dominates rare earth production (60%+) AND processing (85%+). Even minerals mined in Africa/USA must be processed in China. REE export controls deployed as trade weapon against Japan (2010), USA (2023-2025). Invisible chokepoint: halt processing = global tech manufacturing stalls.
properties.wounding_strategy: Supply Chain Chokepoint: Control not just mines but processing. Even if rivals mine REEs domestically, they cannot use them without Chinese processing capacity. Zero military engagement required.

properties.attack_risk: CRITICAL — Armed groups control mining areas; China buying up concessions
properties.warfare_type: Proxy Conflict + Economic Coercion + Resource War
properties.conflict_status: ACTIVE WAR — M23 offensive 2024-2025; Goma besieged
properties.summary: DRC produces 70% of world cobalt — irreplaceable for EV batteries and military electronics. Armed groups and Chinese state companies control extraction. M23 rebel offensive 2024-2025 besieging Goma in eastern DRC. Congo conflict trap (1964-present): 18,888 cumulative deaths.
properties.wounding_strategy: Mineral Neurology Attack: Weaponize local labor unions, fund eco-terrorist sabotage, acquire port terminals where cobalt leaves Africa (Beira, Dar es Salaam). Starve Western EV and defense tech without firing a shot.

properties.attack_risk: MEDIUM — Political instability; lithium nationalization risk
properties.conflict_status: STABLE — Lithium nationalization policy 2023; geopolitical tension with Bolivia/Argentina over 'Lithium Triangle'
properties.summary: Chile holds world's largest lithium reserves and is top copper producer. Atacama Salt Flat contains 40%+ of global lithium. 'Lithium Triangle' (Chile+Bolivia+Argentina) controls 60%+ of global lithium reserves. EV transition makes this the new oil.
properties.wounding_strategy: Lithium Triangle Siege: Fund eco-sabotage, labor union weaponization, or port terminal acquisition across the corridor. Appears as domestic labor disputes — no direct military attribution.

properties.attack_risk: LOW-MEDIUM — China is top trading partner; economic coercion risk
properties.conflict_status: TENSION — China-Australia trade war 2020-2023 (barley, wine, coal bans). Partial normalization 2023+
properties.summary: World's largest lithium producer. Also dominates iron ore (Pilbara) and uranium. China applied economic coercive pressure 2020-2023 after Australia called for COVID inquiry — proof of concept for mineral-linked economic warfare.
properties.wounding_strategy: Economic Dependency Leverage: Australia exports 80%+ of resources to China. Trade bans on barley, wine, coal demonstrated how mineral dependency creates economic warfare leverage without military action.

properties.threat_actor: Russia + China (cyber targeting of mining infrastructure)
properties.conflict_status: STABLE — Key potash supplier (40%+ global); uranium for nuclear fuel
properties.summary: Major potash supplier (global food security dependency) and uranium producer. Saskatchewan potash mines supply fertilizer to 80+ countries. Russia (formerly Belarus+Russia = 70% global potash) has used potash exports as geopolitical tool — Canada is critical Western alternative.
properties.wounding_strategy: Agricultural Security Leverage: Control potash = control global food production. Russia's 2022 Belarus sanctions created 30% potash shortfall. Canada filling gap but vulnerable to cyber attacks on mining infrastructure.

properties.attack_risk: MEDIUM — South China Sea proximity; Chinese investment dependency
properties.threat_actor: China (investment dependency) + South China Sea territorial pressure
properties.conflict_status: TENSION — South China Sea disputes with China; massive Chinese nickel investment creating dependency
properties.summary: Dominates global nickel mining (48%). Critical for EV batteries (nickel-manganese-cobalt chemistry). Indonesia banned raw nickel ore exports 2019 — forced processing onshore, China built processing plants creating new dependency. South China Sea territorial disputes with China ongoing.
properties.wounding_strategy: Downstream Processing Capture: China invested $30B+ in Indonesian nickel smelters. Indonesia gets jobs; China controls processed output. Creates invisible chokepoint — Indonesia mines it, China determines global supply.

properties.attack_risk: LOW-MEDIUM — Domestic production; Chinese REE processing dependency
properties.conflict_status: STRATEGIC COMPETITION — Rebuilding domestic REE processing; Inflation Reduction Act critical mineral incentives
properties.summary: Key North American copper and REE producer but critically dependent on Chinese REE processing. Mountain Pass mine (CA) produces REEs but ships to China for processing — an absurd dependency. IRA 2022 investing $370B to rebuild domestic critical mineral supply chains.
properties.wounding_strategy: Processing Dependency: Even domestically mined REEs must go to China for processing. Rebuilding US processing = 5-10 year lag. Current vulnerability window being exploited in US-China trade war.

properties.attack_risk: MEDIUM — Political instability; Chinese lithium concession competition
properties.threat_actor: China vs USA competition for lithium concessions
properties.conflict_status: VOLATILE — Coup attempt 2024; competing Chinese/Western lithium contracts; Salar de Uyuni exploitation stalled
properties.summary: World's largest lithium reserves (Salar de Uyuni salt flat). Strategic partner competition between China and West for concession rights. Political instability (coup attempt 2024) creates exploitation uncertainty. Landlocked — dependent on Chilean/Peruvian ports.
properties.wounding_strategy: Landlocked Resource Trap: Bolivia can't export without Chile/Peru ports. Diplomatic pressure on neighboring countries can effectively embargo Bolivian lithium. Coup instability enables concession renegotiation.

properties.attack_risk: HIGH — Territorial dispute; Polisario Front active; Algeria backing rebels
properties.threat_actor: Algeria + Polisario Front vs Morocco + USA/France backing
properties.conflict_status: DISPUTED — Morocco controls 80%; Polisario Front resuming armed operations 2020+; Algeria arming Polisario
properties.summary: Morocco (including Western Sahara) controls 72% of global phosphate reserves — critical for all agriculture globally. No phosphate = no fertilizer = mass famine risk. Active territorial dispute with Polisario Front (backed by Algeria). Black Swan: proxy conflict here triggers global food price cascade.
properties.wounding_strategy: Agricultural Neurology: Control phosphate = control global food production. Polisario Front conflict or blockade → fertilizer price collapse → famine cascades in rival nations within one harvest cycle. Zero kinetic engagement with target.
